title: Your Homeownership Journey — From Offer to Closing in BC
metaDescription: An end-to-end overview of buying a home in Greater Vancouver — from pre-qualification through subject removal, closing and your first renewal.
intro: Most buyers Google individual steps as they hit them. Here's the whole journey on one page — what happens, when, and what your mortgage broker does at each stage.

body.heading: Stage 1: Plan (3–18 months before purchase)
paragraphs: Start with a free pre-qualification to find your realistic price range. This is when down-payment plans get built — FHSA contributions, RRSP HBP positioning, paying down debts that hurt your TDS ratio, fixing credit reporting errors. Three months of focus here can shift your maximum mortgage by $50,000–$100,000.

body.heading: Stage 2: Pre-approval (1–4 months before)
paragraphs: Move to a fully underwritten pre-approval with a 90–120 day rate hold. Your realtor now has a real number to shop with, and you're protected against rate increases during the search.

body.heading: Stage 3: Offer and subject removal (typically 5–10 days)
paragraphs: Once you find the home, your offer goes in subject to financing, inspection, title review and (for stratas) document review. The financing subject period — typically 5–7 business days — is when I confirm the lender's appraisal of the specific property and remove the financing condition. In hot Greater Vancouver markets, some buyers go subject-free; this is only safe with airtight pre-approval and an appraisal-waived lender product.

body.heading: Stage 4: Closing (typically 30–90 days after subject removal)
paragraphs: I coordinate with your real-estate lawyer or notary, the lender's funding team, and the seller's lawyer through closing. You sign mortgage documents about a week before completion. On completion day, funds advance, title transfers, and you get keys.

body.heading: Stage 5: First renewal (typically 5 years later)
paragraphs: I keep your file on a renewal calendar. About 120 days before your term ends, we re-shop the market — you'll get a clear comparison of your lender's renewal offer vs. what other lenders are pricing. This single conversation typically saves clients $5,000–$25,000 over the next term.

faqs.question: How long does the whole journey take?
faqs.answer: From first call to closing day: anywhere from 4 weeks (motivated buyer with documents ready) to 18 months (early planner). The journey itself isn't the bottleneck — finding the right home usually is.

faqs.question: Do you stay involved after closing?
faqs.answer: Yes. I track your renewal date, watch market conditions, and reach out 120 days before your term ends with a renewal-shopping plan. I'm also available between renewals if your situation changes (refinance, second property, equity take-out).

faqs.question: What's the most common mistake at each stage?
faqs.answer: Stage 1: not building credit early enough. Stage 2: skipping pre-approval and assuming a pre-qualification is enough. Stage 3: agreeing to subject-free offers without confirming appraisal. Stage 4: making big purchases or job changes between approval and closing. Stage 5: signing the renewal letter without negotiating.
